methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $68M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Emerald AI is a Seed-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Varun Sivaram that has raised $68M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of 9 private companies in the Climate & Energy sector, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, alongside one public comparable (LNZA). However, the narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation profiles to support a reliable peer-median multiple approach.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation anchor. Using total raised ($68M) as a proxy for post-seed valuation and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $170M. This method reflects historical patterns in frontier tech progression but relies on the assumption that $68M raised approximates the company's recent post-money valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $144M to $195M (median $170M), with $170M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer comparables, which limits cross-check validation, and reliance on a single method. Any future assessment would benefit from expanded comparable data at matched stages or more granular revenue/milestone traction metrics if available.
